title: Indoor Room Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The indoor room temperature thermistor is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error E1 indicates the room temperature sensor in the indoor unit has an open circuit, short circuit, or is reading out of range values.
symptoms: AC runs nonstop without reaching set temperature

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at the breaker for 5 minutes, then restart to clear transient errors.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front panel of the indoor unit. Find the room sensor near the air intake and ensure the connector is secure.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure with a multimeter. At 25C expect approximately 10 kOhms.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If readings are out of range, replace with a compatible NTC thermistor.

whenToCallPro: If the indoor PCB is suspected faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Chigo AC E1 room temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: What does E1 mean on Chigo AC?
faq.a: E1 means the room temperature sensor in the indoor unit is faulty and needs checking or replacement.

faq.q: Can I use the AC with E1?
faq.a: The AC may run but cannot regulate temperature properly.

faq.q: Where is the room sensor?
faq.a: It is typically near the air return grille inside the indoor unit.

title: Indoor Coil Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The evaporator coil temperature sensor is reading abnormally.
fullDescription: Error E2 means the indoor evaporator coil thermistor has failed, preventing proper coil freeze protection and temperature control.

fixSteps.title: Reset the AC
fixSteps.instruction: Power off at breaker for 5 minutes. Restart and monitor.

fixSteps.title: Locate and inspect sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Open the indoor unit and find the thermistor clipped to the evaporator coil. Check attachment and connector.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure sensor resistance. At room temperature, expect about 10 kOhms. Replace if abnormal.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove old sensor, clip new one securely to coil, reconnect wiring.

whenToCallPro: If coil icing persists after sensor replacement, indicating a refrigerant issue.
videoSearchQuery: Chigo AC E2 coil sensor error fix

faq.q: Why is my Chigo AC coil freezing?
faq.a: A faulty coil sensor (E2) can prevent the AC from running its defrost protection, leading to ice buildup.

faq.q: Is E2 related to low refrigerant?
faq.a: E2 is a sensor fault, but low refrigerant can also cause coil icing as a separate issue.

faq.q: How do I prevent coil icing?
faq.a: Keep filters clean, ensure proper airflow, and have the sensor replaced if faulty.

title: Outdoor Coil Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The outdoor condenser coil temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: Error E3 means the outdoor condenser coil thermistor is providing abnormal readings, affecting condenser protection and defrost operation.

causes.cause: Wiring damaged by animals or weather

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at the breaker for 5 minutes and restart.

fixSteps.title: Inspect outdoor sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Find the sensor clipped to the condenser coil. Check for corrosion, damage, or detachment.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure sensor resistance. Expect approximately 10 kOhms at 25C.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install new sensor, clip securely to coil, route wire safely.

whenToCallPro: If outdoor unit access is unsafe.
videoSearchQuery: Chigo AC E3 outdoor sensor error fix

faq.q: Can weather damage the outdoor sensor?
faq.a: Yes, outdoor sensors are exposed to rain, sun, and temperature extremes that degrade them over time.

faq.q: How long do outdoor sensors last?
faq.a: Typically 5-10 years, shorter in harsh environments.

faq.q: Does E3 affect cooling?
faq.a: Yes, without condenser monitoring the system may not protect itself properly.

title: Compressor Discharge Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The compressor discharge temperature has exceeded safe limits.
fullDescription: Error E4 triggers when the compressor discharge temperature exceeds the safe threshold, protecting the compressor from thermal damage.
symptoms: Very hot air from outdoor unit

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Wash the outdoor condenser coil with a garden hose to remove accumulated dirt and debris.

fixSteps.title: Clear obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove any plants, covers, or objects within 12 inches of the outdoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Check outdoor fan
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the outdoor fan is spinning. If not, check the fan motor and capacitor.

fixSteps.title: Call for refrigerant check
fixSteps.instruction: If the condenser is clean and airflow is good, a refrigerant check by a certified technician is required.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, indicating refrigerant or compressor problems.
videoSearchQuery: Chigo AC E4 compressor overheat error fix

faq.q: Is E4 serious?
faq.a: Yes, repeated compressor overheating can cause permanent damage. Address the root cause promptly.

faq.q: Can a dirty filter cause E4?
faq.a: A dirty indoor filter reduces airflow and can indirectly contribute to compressor stress.

faq.q: How hot is too hot for a compressor?
faq.a: Discharge temperatures above 120C are considered dangerous for most residential compressors.

title: Compressor Overcurrent Protection
shortDescription: The compressor is drawing more current than allowed by the protection circuit.
fullDescription: Error E5 indicates the compressor current sensor has detected excessive amperage, triggering protection to prevent compressor and wiring damage.

causes.cause: Dirty condenser causing high head pressure

fixSteps.title: Check power supply voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the supply voltage at the outdoor unit. It should be within 10% of rated voltage (220V or 230V).

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser coil
fixSteps.instruction: Thoroughly wash the outdoor condenser to ensure proper heat rejection.

fixSteps.title: Test compressor capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Discharge and test the capacitor. Replace if capacitance is more than 10% below rated value.

fixSteps.title: Measure compressor amps
fixSteps.instruction: Use a clamp meter on the compressor wire. Compare to the nameplate rating.

whenToCallPro: If the compressor itself is drawing excessive current even with a clean condenser and good capacitor.
videoSearchQuery: Chigo AC E5 compressor overcurrent error fix

faq.q: Can low voltage cause E5?
faq.a: Yes, low voltage forces the compressor to draw more current, potentially triggering overcurrent protection.

faq.q: Is it safe to reset and try again?
faq.a: You can try once after cleaning the condenser, but repeated resets without fixing the cause can damage the compressor.

faq.q: What voltage should my AC receive?
faq.a: Most split ACs need 220-240V. Anything below 200V can cause problems.

title: Communication Error Between Indoor and Outdoor Units
shortDescription: The indoor and outdoor units have lost communication with each other.
fullDescription: Error E6 means the serial communication link between the indoor and outdoor unit control boards is interrupted.
symptoms: Indoor fan runs but no cooling
Error appears within minutes of startup

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at breaker for 5 minutes. Restart both units.

fixSteps.title: Check interconnect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the cable between indoor and outdoor units. Look for damaged or disconnected wires.

fixSteps.title: Tighten terminals
fixSteps.instruction: Check and tighten all terminal connections on both the indoor and outdoor unit boards.

fixSteps.title: Test wire continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect communication wires at both ends and test continuity with a multimeter.

whenToCallPro: If the PCB on either unit has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Chigo AC E6 communication error indoor outdoor fix

faq.q: What connects the indoor and outdoor units?
faq.a: A multi-conductor cable carries power and communication signals between the two units.

faq.q: Can rodents cause E6?
faq.a: Yes, rodents can chew through communication wires, especially where cables pass through walls.

faq.q: Is E6 common?
faq.a: E6 is one of the more common errors, usually caused by simple wiring issues.

title: Outdoor Fan Motor Protection
shortDescription: The outdoor fan motor has triggered its protection circuit.
fullDescription: Error E7 indicates the outdoor unit fan motor is not running correctly, has stalled, or is drawing excessive current.

fixSteps.title: Remove debris
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the AC. Remove any leaves, sticks, or objects blocking the fan blade.

fixSteps.title: Spin fan manually
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, try spinning the fan by hand. It should rotate smoothly. Resistance means bad bearings.

fixSteps.title: Test fan capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Test the fan motor capacitor with a multimeter. Replace if out of spec.

fixSteps.title: Replace fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If motor is seized or burned, replace the entire fan motor assembly.

videoSearchQuery: Chigo AC E7 outdoor fan motor error fix

faq.q: Can I run my AC if the outdoor fan is not working?
faq.a: No, the outdoor fan is critical for heat rejection. The compressor will overheat without it.

faq.q: What causes fan motor failure?
faq.a: Common causes include worn bearings, power surges, and debris damage.

faq.q: How much does a fan motor cost?
faq.a: Replacement fan motors for Chigo ACs typically cost $25-$65.

title: EEPROM Memory Error
shortDescription: The control board EEPROM chip has a data error.
fullDescription: Error F1 indicates the EEPROM memory on the indoor PCB, which stores configuration parameters, has a read or write fault.

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at breaker for 10 minutes. This extended reset may allow EEPROM to reinitialize.

fixSteps.title: Install a surge protector
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the AC to a surge protector to prevent future power surge damage.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the PCB
fixSteps.instruction: Open the indoor unit and look for burned components or damage on the PCB.

fixSteps.title: Replace the PCB
fixSteps.instruction: If the EEPROM cannot be recovered, the indoor PCB needs replacement.

videoSearchQuery: Chigo AC F1 EEPROM error PCB fix

faq.q: What is EEPROM?
faq.a: EEPROM is a memory chip that stores the AC settings and calibration data permanently.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F1?
faq.a: Yes, power surges are the most common cause of EEPROM data corruption.

faq.q: Is the PCB expensive?
faq.a: Indoor PCBs for Chigo typically cost $35-$90.
